message:
🐛 Fix install missions for Kyverno, Trivy, and Kubescape (#350)

Tested all three install missions on an OpenShift cluster (vllm-d).
Found and fixed several issues:

Kyverno:
- Fix Helm version: use chart version 3.7.1, not app version v1.17.1
- Change sample policy from enforce to Audit to avoid breaking workloads
- Add OpenShift SCC instructions for all service accounts

Trivy:
- Add CRD verification note (CRDs use aquasecurity.github.io group)
- Add ignoreUnfixed flag to reduce noise
- Document ephemeral scan pods behavior

Kubescape:
- Add --set capabilities.continuousScan=enable for periodic scanning
- Add OpenShift SCC instructions (privileged for node-agent, nonroot-v2 for others)
- Add troubleshooting step for OpenShift SCC issues

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -16,7 +16,7 @@
       },
       {
         "title": "Install Kubescape Operator",
-        "description": "Install the Kubescape operator using Helm. This command will create a namespace called 'kubescape' if it doesn't exist and install the operator in that namespace.
```bash
helm install kubescape-operator kubescape/kubescape-operator --namespace kubescape --create-namespace --version 1.30.5
```"
+        "description": "Install the Kubescape operator using Helm. This command will create a namespace called 'kubescape' if it doesn't exist and install the operator in that namespace.
```bash
helm install kubescape-operator kubescape/kubescape-operator --namespace kubescape --create-namespace --version 1.30.5 --set capabilities.continuousScan=enable
```

**OpenShift clusters:** Kubescape pods require Security Context Constraints (SCCs). After install, grant appropriate SCCs:
```bash
# node-agent needs privileged SCC (hostPID, hostPath access)
kubectl create clusterrolebinding kubescape-scc-node-agent --clusterrole=system:openshift:scc:privileged --serviceaccount=kubescape:node-agent

# Other components run as UID 65532 (nonroot)
for sa in kubescape kubevuln operator storage; do
  kubectl create clusterrolebinding "kubescape-scc-${sa}" --clusterrole=system:openshift:scc:nonroot-v2 --serviceaccount="kubescape:${sa}"
done

# Restart all deployments and daemonset
kubectl -n kubescape rollout restart deployment kubescape kubevuln operator storage
kubectl -n kubescape rollout restart daemonset node-agent
```"
       },
       {
         "title": "Verify the installation",
@@ -75,13 +75,17 @@
       {
         "title": "Resource limits not applied",
         "description": "If resource limits are not applied, ensure that you edited the correct deployment and that the syntax is correct.
```bash
kubectl get deployment kubescape-operator -n kubescape -o yaml
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Pods stuck in Pending on OpenShift",
+        "description": "On OpenShift clusters, Kubescape pods may fail to start due to Security Context Constraints (SCCs). Check events for SCC errors:
```bash
kubectl get events -n kubescape --field-selector reason=FailedCreate
```
Fix by granting appropriate SCCs — see the OpenShift note in the install step."
       }
     ],
     "resolution": {
       "summary": "A successful installation of Kubescape will have the operator running in the 'kubescape' namespace with all pods in a 'Running' state. You can verify this by checking the pod status with `kubectl get pods -n kubescape`.",
       "codeSnippets": [
         "helm repo add kubescape https://kubescape.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update",
-        "helm install kubescape-operator kubescape/kubescape-operator --namespace kubescape --create-namespace --version 1.30.5",
+        "helm install kubescape-operator kubescape/kubescape-operator --namespace kubescape --create-namespace --version 1.30.5 --set capabilities.continuousScan=enable",
         "kubectl get pods -n kubescape",
         "kubectl edit deployment kubescape-operator -n kubescape
# Add the following under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     memory: "512Mi"
#     cpu: "500m""
       ]

@@ -16,7 +16,7 @@
       },
       {
         "title": "Install Kyverno using Helm",
-        "description": "Next, install Kyverno in your Kubernetes cluster with the following command. This will create the necessary namespace and install the latest version of Kyverno:
```bash
helm install kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --create-namespace --version v1.17.1
```"
+        "description": "Next, install Kyverno in your Kubernetes cluster with the following command. This will create the necessary namespace and install Kyverno v1.17.1:
```bash
helm install kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --create-namespace --version 3.7.1
```

**Note:** The chart version is `3.7.1` (app version v1.17.1). Do not use `--version v1.17.1` — that is the app version, not the chart version.

**OpenShift clusters:** Kyverno pods require Security Context Constraints (SCCs). After install, grant `nonroot-v2` SCC to all Kyverno service accounts:
```bash
for sa in kyverno-admission-controller kyverno-background-controller kyverno-cleanup-controller kyverno-reports-controller; do
  kubectl create clusterrolebinding "kyverno-scc-${sa}" --clusterrole=system:openshift:scc:nonroot-v2 --serviceaccount="kyverno:${sa}"
done
kubectl -n kyverno rollout restart deployment kyverno-admission-controller kyverno-background-controller kyverno-cleanup-controller kyverno-reports-controller
```"
       },
       {
         "title": "Verify the installation",
@@ -28,14 +28,14 @@
       },
       {
         "title": "Test a sample policy",
-        "description": "To ensure Kyverno is working as expected, you can apply a sample policy. Create a YAML file named `sample-policy.yaml` and apply it:
```yaml
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-non-root
spec:
  validationFailureAction: enforce
  rules:
    - name: require-non-root
      match:
        resources:
          kinds:
            - Pod
      validate:
        message: "Run as non-root user"
        pattern:
          spec:
            securityContext:
              runAsUser: 1000
```
Then apply it:
```bash
kubectl apply -f sample-policy.yaml
```"
+        "description": "To ensure Kyverno is working as expected, apply a sample audit policy. **Important:** Use `validationFailureAction: Audit` (not `Enforce`) to avoid breaking workloads during initial testing.

Create a YAML file named `sample-policy.yaml` and apply it:
```yaml
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-labels
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  rules:
    - name: check-team-label
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
            - Pod
      validate:
        message: "The label 'team' is required."
        pattern:
          metadata:
            labels:
              team: "?*"
```
Then apply it:
```bash
kubectl apply -f sample-policy.yaml
kubectl get clusterpolicy
```"
       }
     ],
     "resolution": {
       "summary": "A successful installation of Kyverno will show the Kyverno pods in a running state when you execute `kubectl get pods --namespace kyverno`. You can also verify the configuration by applying a sample policy and checking for enforcement.",
       "codeSnippets": [
         "helm repo add kyverno https://kyverno.github.io/kyverno
helm repo update",
-        "helm install kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --create-namespace --version v1.17.1",
+        "helm install kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --create-namespace --version 3.7.1",
         "kubectl get pods --namespace kyverno",
         "helm upgrade kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno --set podSecurityStandard=baseline",
         "apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-non-root
spec:
  validationFailureAction: enforce
  rules:
    - name: require-non-root
      match:
        resources:
          kinds:
            - Pod
      validate:
        message: "Run as non-root user"
        pattern:
          spec:
            securityContext:
              runAsUser: 1000"

@@ -12,11 +12,11 @@
     "steps": [
       {
         "title": "Install Trivy Operator using Helm",
-        "description": "Add the Aqua Security Helm repository and install the Trivy Operator. Ensure you are using Helm version 3.0 or higher.

```bash
helm repo add aquasecurity https://aquasecurity.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
helm install trivy-operator aquasecurity/trivy-operator --version 0.23.0 --namespace trivy --create-namespace
```"
+        "description": "Add the Aqua Security Helm repository and install the Trivy Operator. Ensure you are using Helm version 3.0 or higher.

```bash
helm repo add aquasecurity https://aquasecurity.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
helm install trivy-operator aquasecurity/trivy-operator --version 0.23.0 --namespace trivy --create-namespace --set trivy.ignoreUnfixed=true
```

**Note:** The Trivy Operator will immediately begin scanning workloads after installation. Scan pods (`scan-vulnerabilityreport-*`) are ephemeral and will complete on their own."
       },
       {
         "title": "Verify Installation",
-        "description": "Check if the Trivy Operator is running successfully in the cluster.

```bash
kubectl get pods -n trivy
kubectl get deployment trivy-operator -n trivy
```"
+        "description": "Check if the Trivy Operator is running successfully in the cluster.

```bash
kubectl get pods -n trivy
kubectl get deployment trivy-operator -n trivy
```

Verify CRDs are registered (Trivy CRDs use the `aquasecurity.github.io` API group):
```bash
kubectl get crd | grep aquasecurity
```"
       },
       {
         "title": "Configure RBAC for Trivy Operator",
